Yopal from the Chamber of Commerce, **Second**: Cultural center, **Third**: El Alcaraván Airport, **Four**: Nunchía Avenue | |

(Spanish pronunciation: [[ɟʝoˈpal]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish")) is a municipality and capital city of the [department](/wiki/Departments_of_Colombia "Departments of Colombia") of [Casanare](/wiki/Casanare_Department "Casanare Department") in [Colombia](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia") and the second most populated and important city in the [Orinoquía region](/wiki/Orinoqu%C3%ADa_natural_region "Orinoquía natural region") after [Villavicencio](/wiki/Villavicencio "Villavicencio").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Telefónicas, Telencuestas-Encuestas. ["Cuántos habitantes tiene Yopal, Casanare en 2023"](https://telencuestas.com/censos-de-poblacion/colombia/2023/casanare/yopal). *Telencuestas* (in Spanish). Retrieved 2023-11-07.

["Promedios Climatológicos 1981–2010"](https://web.archive.org/web/20160815025712/http://www.ideam.gov.co/documents/21021/553571/Promedios%2BClimatol%C3%B3gicos%2B%2B1981%2B-%2B2010.xlsx/f28d0b07-1208-4a46-8ccf-bddd70fb4128) (in Spanish). Instituto de Hidrologia Meteorologia y Estudios Ambientales. Archived from [the original](http://www.ideam.gov.co/documents/21021/553571/Promedios+Climatol%C3%B3gicos++1981+-+2010.xlsx/f28d0b07-1208-4a46-8ccf-bddd70fb4128) on 15 August 2016. Retrieved 17 June 2024.

**Yopal** (Spanish pronunciation: [[ɟʝoˈpal]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish")) is a municipality and capital city of the [department](/wiki/Departments_of_Colombia "Departments of Colombia") of [Casanare](/wiki/Casanare_Department "Casanare Department") in [Colombia](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia") and the second most populated and important city in the [Orinoquía region](/wiki/Orinoqu%C3%ADa_natural_region "Orinoquía natural region") after [Villavicencio](/wiki/Villavicencio "Villavicencio").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yopal&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Before the period of the [Spanish colonization of the Americas](/wiki/Spanish_colonization_of_the_Americas "Spanish colonization of the Americas"), the land on which Yopal stands was occupied by the indigenous [Achagua people](/wiki/Achagua_people "Achagua people"). The name Yopal descends from the region's abundance of *[Anadenanthera peregrina](/wiki/Anadenanthera_peregrina "Anadenanthera peregrina")*, often otherwise called yopo.

## Geographic information

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yopal&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geographic information")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/cd/Cerro_buena_vista.jpg/120px-Cerro_buena_vista.jpg)](/wiki/File:Cerro_buena_vista.jpg)

Skyline of Yopal from the Cerro Buena vista (Buena Vista Hill)

* Population: 191.133 inhabitants
* Elevation: 350 metres or 1,150 feet
* Area: 2,771 square kilometres or 1,070 square miles
* Distance from [Bogotá](/wiki/Bogot%C3%A1 "Bogotá"): 387 kilometres or 240 miles
* Median temperature: 26 °C or 78.8 °F

## Born in Yopal

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yopal&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Born in Yopal")]

* [Camila Avella](/wiki/Camila_Avella "Camila Avella"), Model and [Miss Universe Colombia 2023](/wiki/Miss_Universe_Colombia_2023 "Miss Universe Colombia 2023")
* [Jeffry Romero](/wiki/Jeffry_Romero "Jeffry Romero"), professional cyclist

## Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yopal&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Climate")]

Yopal has a [tropical monsoon climate](/wiki/Tropical_monsoon_climate "Tropical monsoon climate") (Köppen *Am*). Although it borders closely on a [tropical savanna climate](/wiki/Tropical_savanna_climate "Tropical savanna climate") (*Aw*), Yopal’s climate is much more typical of a tropical monsoon climate in having a short but distinct [dry season](/wiki/Dry_season "Dry season") that covers the months of December to March, and a very long [wet season](/wiki/Wet_season "Wet season") covering the remaining eight months.
